era: 1984-1997 (first period), 2010-2017 (reunion). Grunge/Metal. E=9. University District, Seattle. Sub Pop to SST to A&M. First Seattle grunge band on a major label.
members: Chris Cornell (vocals/guitar, born July 20 1964 Seattle WA, died May 18 2017 Detroit MI, age 52)
Kim Thayil (guitar, born September 4 1960 Seattle WA)
Matt Cameron (drums, born November 28 1962 San Diego CA)
key_albums: Screaming Life EP (1987, Sub Pop) -- Sub Pop debut
Ultramega OK (1988, SST) -- full-length debut, Grammy nomination Best Metal Performance
Louder Than Love (1989, A&M) -- first major label record, first Seattle grunge band on major
Badmotorfinger (1991, A&M) -- critical peak, Rusty Cage, Outshined, Jesus Christ Pose; same date as Nevermind
Superunknown (1994, A&M) -- commercial peak, Black Hole Sun, Spoonman, Fell on Black Days, #1 US, 2 Grammys, 9M worldwide
Down on the Upside (1996, A&M) -- final pre-hiatus album
King Animal (2012, Universal) -- reunion studio record

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST SEATTLE GRUNGE BAND ON A MAJOR LABEL. A&M Records signing 1989: two years before Nirvana/DGC, three years before Pearl Jam/Epic. The signing demonstrated the path was possible. Made the scene's commercial breakthrough imaginable before Nevermind proved it at scale.
theory_nodes.description: Soundgarden as the first grunge band to demonstrate the Sub Pop-to-major path.

theory_nodes.concept: Chris Cornell (July 20 1964 - May 18 2017). Four-octave voice range. Control: whisper-to-scream within a phrase, full-voice sustained high notes. Lyrical concerns: existential dread, surface vs. depth, alienation. Died May 18 2017 Detroit after Fox Theatre concert, age 52. The artistry is the lasting record.
theory_nodes.description: Cornell's voice, artistry, and death documented with factual respect.

theory_nodes.concept: Kim Thayil: open tunings, custom alternate tunings, detuning below standard. Harmonic strangeness: minor-major sevenths, suspended fourths, modal harmony. Volume control as expressive parameter. The most theoretically sophisticated guitar approach in the grunge canon.
theory_nodes.description: Thayil's guitar approach as grunge's most theoretically sophisticated.

theory_nodes.concept: Badmotorfinger + Nevermind released same day: September 24 1991. Two most important grunge records of 1991 entered the market simultaneously. Nevermind outsold Badmotorfinger dramatically in months following; Badmotorfinger's breakthrough came gradually.
theory_nodes.description: Badmotorfinger-Nevermind same-day release and different commercial trajectories.

theory_nodes.concept: Superunknown (March 8 1994): #1 US, 9M worldwide, 2 Grammys. Black Hole Sun (alternate tuning, odd phrasing, surreal video). Spoonman (7/4 time). Released 28 days before Cobain's death April 5 1994. The commercial peak of grunge and its ending crossed in March-April 1994.
theory_nodes.description: Superunknown's commercial peak and its temporal proximity to Cobain's death.

theory_nodes.concept: HIRO YAMAMOTO THREAD: original bassist 1984-1989, Screaming Life through Louder Than Love. Left for Truly (degrees 220, 302). Personnel thread: Soundgarden (312) -> Yamamoto -> Truly (220/302) -> Capitol Hill post-grunge. The engine cross-reference runs through biography.
theory_nodes.description: Yamamoto as personnel thread connecting Soundgarden to Truly across three engine degrees.

theory_nodes.concept: TEMPLE OF THE DOG (degree 225): Cornell + Cameron (Soundgarden) + Gossard + Ament + McCready + Vedder (pre-Pearl Jam). Andrew Wood tribute 1991. Hunger Strike: first Cornell-Vedder duet. The Soundgarden-Pearl Jam connection at its most direct and musically significant.
theory_nodes.description: Temple of the Dog as the Soundgarden-Pearl Jam connection and Andrew Wood memorial.

cross_references.relationship: Cornell's Andrew Wood tribute. Cornell + Cameron + pre-Pearl Jam lineup. Hunger Strike: first Cornell-Vedder duet. The Soundgarden-Pearl Jam direct connection.
Yamamoto was Soundgarden's original bassist 1984-1989. Left to form Truly. Personnel thread connecting Soundgarden to Capitol Hill post-grunge across three engine degrees.
Both at E=9 in the Summit. Badmotorfinger and Nevermind released same day September 24 1991. Cornell and Cobain were Seattle peers. Both lost to deaths. Both handled with respect.
Melvins in Soundgarden influence network. The sludge metal approach informed Soundgarden's heaviest material. Shared Seattle extreme rock ecosystem.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. Full coastline impact resonance paired with Soundgarden's full-band sonic impact. Black Hole Sun's ominous beautiful weight and the Pacific winter storm's ominous beautiful power. Both at E=9.
